_comment: Day-1 smoke test payload. Per operator directive (transcript 2026-04-28 line 1664-1704): 'no config and just the USFM for like the BSB and see what comes out'. PTXprint falls back to bundled Charis when no config_files / fonts are supplied (Day-1 inherits upstream Dockerfile's font bundle; Day-2 strips and forces explicit font payloads per session-3 C-007).
_source: BSB Gospel of John from usfm-bible/examples.bsb (a USFM-3.1-correct exemplar fork of the Berean Standard Bible — public domain, same licensing as upstream BSB). URL is pinned to commit 48a9feb71f0a66f9b8f418b11ae25b7ad2e49a0d for reproducibility. The repo lays the file out as 44JHNBSB.usfm (lowercase ext); we fetch those bytes and save them as 44JHNBSB.SFM (uppercase) on the container, which is the canonical Paratext filename PTXprint expects. JHN is book number 44 in the canonical USFM book-id order (41=MAT, 42=MRK, 43=LUK, 44=JHN); the previous placeholder had 43, which would be Luke.
_todo: Confirm config_name default behavior with an empty config_files map; PTXprint may need a minimal [project] section + Settings.xml to find the source.
If PTXprint refuses a fully-empty config, fall back to a single-key cfg under config_files (or include the project's Settings.xml / BookNames.xml from the same upstream commit).
